products.name: Monarch 5 Engine
products.descriptor: Microturbine turboprop engine for 300–2,500 lb UAV propulsion

evidence.snippet: The above video shows our Monarch 5 Engine serving as the prime mover for a 480lb take-off weight TigerShark drone.

The Monarch 5 engine demonstration with the 480-pound TigerShark drone platform validated our system’s capabilities. Flight tests confirmed significant performance improvements over reciprocating engines.

products.name: Turbine Aerospace Systems
products.descriptor: Microturbine propulsion family for drones from 300 to 2,500 lb takeoff weight

evidence.snippet: UAVT’s Turbine Aerospace Systems are sized for drones ranging in take-off weight from 300 to 2,500 pounds. UAVT uses propellers to produce propulsion and can be configured for either horizontal or vertical flight.

UAVT’s Turbine Aerospace Systems power drones ranging from 350 to 2,500 pounds takeoff weight. Industry studies project microturbine engine demand for aerospace applications will exceed $4.6 billion by 2030.

products.name: APEX (Advanced Portable Expeditionary) Generator
products.descriptor: 25 kW man-portable microturbine generator for military and emergency use

evidence.snippet: UAVT’s **25kW** generator sets offer extraordinary size and weight advantages over traditional diesel units: 75% lighter and 50% smaller, delivering critical logistical benefits. Specialized military operations requiring substantial electricity and rapid mobility will be early adopters of UAVT’s APEX (Advanced Portable Expeditionary) generators.
Subsequent to demonstrations for various US Department of Defense organizations, APEX prototypes will be field tested in late 2025.

products.name: APEX 30
products.descriptor: Advanced Portable Expeditionary turbine generator prototype variant

evidence.snippet: Subsequent to demonstrations for various US Department of Defense organizations, APEX prototypes will be field tested in late 2025.

products.name: Aerospace Range Extender
products.descriptor: Turbine-based hybrid range extender for Group 3–4 battery-electric drones

evidence.snippet:
Recognizing this power density limitation five years ago, UAVT developed a high-speed generator to replace the gearbox used in propeller applications.
The result is a high-power-to-weight ratio system that provides sufficient electrical power in a compact, lightweight package to serve as a hybrid range extender for larger drones that are required to carry meaningful payloads more than a short distance.

products.name: M5IB Generator
products.descriptor: Microturbine generator set built on the Monarch 5 engine core

evidence.snippet: The UAVT core engine that has been used with a propeller for propulsion applications, anchors our Generator Set design. We use a proprietary generator in this configuration which operates at the same 100,000 RPM output as the engine. The resulting electrical power is easily conditioned to standard AC or DC output.

products.descriptor: Turbine engine variant for UAV propulsion applications
